svg.title: iCargo Customer capabilities overview
svg.desc: Customer portal capability view showing cards, package list, SKU mapping, store-order fetch, carrier choice, single and multi-order booking, batch actions, and rules.
svg.text: iCargo Customer: What The Portal Actually Did

text.tspan: This is the customer-side story to tell in interviews: customers could manage cards and package data,
fetch store orders, choose carriers, and turn one or many orders into shipments from the same portal.

svg.text: BOOKING AND CARRIER CHOICE
Customer Shipping Flows
• single booking page, quick import, and return shipment flows
• create shipments from one order or many selected orders
• choose services during booking from the same carrier family
DHL
UKMAIL
DSV
UPS
PALLETWAYS
FEDEX
NORSK
DPD
ZAJEL
XPRESS
ACCOUNT AND PACKAGE SETUP
Customer Self-Service Tools
• add card, prepay, add fund, and pay-invoice style actions
• package list, package types, and package edit flows
• SKU mapping connected to package data
• addresses, users, printer setup, and account utilities
• settings customers usually prepare before booking
STORE ORDERS AND COMMERCE
Connected Commerce Workbench
• cart-settings and shop connection style pages
• fetch remote orders from connected stores
• refresh, filter, and packaging-slip actions on order lists
• evidence of Shopify, WooCommerce, eBay, and similar flows
• use order data as the starting point for shipment booking
BATCH, MULTI-ORDER, AND RULES
Operational Actions From The Portal
• create shipment from a normal single-order booking page
• create shipment for multiple selected orders
• batch orders and batch action flows
• rule-based workflow configuration on the customer side
• quotes, saved quotes, and invoice-linked actions
Strong Interview Summary

text.tspan: iCargo Customer was not just a booking page. It was a customer workbench where users could add cards,
manage package lists, fetch Shopify or WooCommerce orders, choose carriers, create shipments
from one or many orders, and use batch, rules, quotes, and invoice flows.
